characters.availability: Present in all chapters as POV or active participant. Crosses threshold in Ch 18; present in sub-Ledger from Ch 18 onward.

key_relationships.maren: lost love — crossed threshold eight years ago

characters.literacy_languages: Fully literate. Speaks the common tongue and some Margins dialect. Can read Hoarded formal script from years of encountering their documents.
characters.occupation_status: Freelance healer and explorer in Spent territory. Takes jobs the Hoarded abandon — healing, rescue, infrastructure repair. Paid in marks or barter.
characters.physical_details: Lean and weathered from forty years of fieldwork. Scarred hands. Moves with the economy of someone who has learned to do everything at minimum effort. Looks older than her age — hard living, sun damage, accumulated injuries healed at minimum cost. Short graying hair kept practical. Strong but not large.

state_transitions.change: Level drops from 33 to 31 after healing job; commits to journey; checks Maren's route journal
Level drops to approximately 28 after outrider fight (3 levels: surgical combat)
Level drops to approximately 22; cracked ribs from glancing hit (6 levels: 4 combat + 2 evasion)
Level drops to approximately 20 (2 levels: Concealment + defensive during escape); consults route journal, Thessa recognizes it
Level drops to approximately 18 (2 levels: small Margins tasks)
Level drops to approximately 16 (2 levels: rescue operation)
Level drops to approximately 14; reveals Maren and acknowledges moral asymmetry of her quest
Level drops to approximately 8 (6 levels: Perception mapping, tunnel navigation, evasion)
Level reaches 0; crosses the threshold to the sub-Ledger
In the sub-Ledger; reunited with Maren

characters.availability: Present from Ch 2 onward. Crosses threshold in Ch 18.

key_relationships.lady_aldraste: mother — loves him, opposes his defection

characters.literacy_languages: Fully literate in Hoarded formal script and common tongue. Educated in history, philosophy, governance — all theoretical. No practical knowledge of the world outside the Vault.
characters.occupation_status: Former Hoarded aristocrat, House Aldraste. No skills, no trade, no practical experience at story start. Heir to one of the major Hoarded families.
characters.physical_details: Tall, well-fed, unblemished — the body of someone who has never exerted himself. Soft hands. Pale from indoor Vault life. As the book progresses, he accumulates bruises, calluses, sunburn, and the general wear of someone learning to use a body for the first time. By late chapters, visibly leaner and harder.

state_transitions.change: Level drops from 300 to approximately 288; leaves the Vault; takes House signet from the gate stand
Level approximately 286 after incidentals during meeting with Vel
Level drops to approximately 245 after wasteful first combat (41 levels)
Level approximately 225 after incidentals through Spent Towns (20 levels over ~6 days); signet draws hostile looks
Level drops to approximately 183 after agent fight (42 levels: defensive perimeter)
Level approximately 178; performs first competent non-ability act (field medicine)
Level drops to approximately 160 (18 levels: defensive abilities during escape from Hoarded representative); first crack in trust after Thessa's revelation
Level drops to approximately 148 (12 levels: incidentals Vel confronts him about — Flame Charms, Strength boosts, Light abilities); resists spending at river
Level drops from 148 to approximately 50 (98 levels: healing sick child 8, breaking enforcement mechanism 20, combat with enforcers 35, infrastructure repair 15, smaller interventions 20)

Level drops from 50 to 31 (19 levels: emergency road-collapse spend 12, incidentals 7); Vel's starting number; discovers Vel's manipulation; alliance fractures; signet taken off hand, considered for discarding, returned to pocket
Level drops from 31 to 25 (6 levels: precision rescue — reinforcement 2, lifting 2, light 1, stabilization 1); briefly comprehends Hoarded impulse toward preservation
Level approximately 18 after accumulated minor spending over several days
Level drops from 18 to 12 (6 levels: tunnel — light 2, reinforcement 3, defensive 1)
Level drops from 12 to 0 (climax: defensive burst saving Vel 5, precision disarm 2, push to threshold 3, final crossing 2); crosses the threshold
In the sub-Ledger; no Ledger, no abilities; signet set on windowsill

characters.availability: POV in Ch 6 and 15. Referenced/intercut in Ch 8, 11, 17. Appears in Ch 18 climax. Intercut coda in Ch 19. Not present in sub-Ledger — remains above the threshold.

key_relationships.kem_aldraste: retrieval target turned subject of empathy

characters.literacy_languages: Fully literate. Expert in Hoarded formal script, legal documentation, and administrative protocol.
characters.occupation_status: Senior Auditor, Hoarded Council. Administrative authority over financial oversight. Assigned to field retrieval of Kem Aldraste — a role entirely outside her experience.
characters.physical_details: Average build maintained by complete inaction — not fit, not unfit, simply unused. Precise posture from decades of administrative propriety. Dark hair kept in formal Hoarded style early; increasingly disheveled as the pursuit continues. By Ch 15, she looks like a person who has been outside for six weeks for the first time.

state_transitions.change: Level drops from 300 to 298; first ability use (Wayfinder); departs the Vault
Level approximately 285; watches combat and begins studying it
Level approximately 270; reports to Lady Aldraste while withholding her transformation; the question forms: does spending make you more?

Level approximately 238; arrives alone; encounters Margins defector with Stillness damage; chooses not to retrieve Kem; warns about Red Reserve and permanent Scour garrison; heads south with a personal question about zero
Level approximately 236 (2 spent on tracking); trailing three hours behind; making incremental decisions toward the Scour
Level drops to approximately 229; burns 7 levels to stabilize the degraded threshold — the essential third contributor; watches the threshold close
At 229; files a false report to protect the sub-Ledger — 'Level-zero termination, no threshold event observed'

characters.availability: Present Ch 7 through Ch 16. Departs before the Scour approach.
characters.literacy_languages: Functionally literate. Speaks common tongue and some trader cant.
characters.occupation_status: Trader running goods between Spent Town settlements. Level 138 at introduction.
characters.physical_details: Stocky, road-worn, tanned. Calloused hands from years of loading cargo. A practical body that has done practical work. After Ch 8, carries a poorly healed wound on his left arm — a gash from silo shrapnel stitched by Kem's inexpert hands.

state_transitions.change: Joins the group as a guide at level 138
Wounded by silo shrapnel — deep gash on left arm
Arm stitched by Kem; functional but poorly healed
Level approximately 130; encounters Margins poverty; makes first non-transactional choice to continue — watching Kem spend for strangers rearranges his understanding of what levels are for
Departs the group at approximately level 120; full departure scene — his daughter is his zero; his arm has healed badly; what the journey looks like from outside

characters.availability: Present only in Ch 10. Referenced in Ch 16.
characters.occupation_status: Settlement trader and community member. Level 78. Spends approximately one level per year on practical tasks.
characters.physical_details: Sturdy, settled. The body of someone who has stopped pushing and started maintaining. Weathered but less worn than Vel — she has been stationary for twelve years. Hands show settlement work: gardening, construction, domestic labor.

state_transitions.change: Confronted by Vel; gives partial route information; her Hoarded deal is exposed; reveals Vel came through years ago asking about high-level defectors; recognizes Maren's route journal

characters.availability: Present in Ch 12. Referenced in Ch 17.
characters.literacy_languages: Minimal literacy — learned practically, not formally.
characters.occupation_status: Former indentured field laborer in Hoarded-owned agriculture. Level 4. Currently subsisting in the Margins.
characters.physical_details: Gaunt, weathered beyond their years. The body of someone spent down through labor — thin, careful in movement, conserving energy in everything they do. Hands roughened by thirty years of field work.

characters.availability: Brief presence in Ch 2. Communication in Ch 11. Referenced in Ch 6, 15.

key_relationships.kem_aldraste: son — loves him, opposes his defection

characters.literacy_languages: Fully literate in all Hoarded scripts.
characters.occupation_status: Matriarch of House Aldraste. Level 300. Hoarded Council influence.
characters.physical_details: Perfectly preserved Hoarded aristocrat. Level 300. The stillness of someone who has never acted — but unlike her husband, she is still fully present behind it. Impeccably maintained appearance.

characters.availability: Present in Ch 2 at the Conservatory Rite. Referenced throughout as Kem's motivation.
characters.occupation_status: Patriarch of House Aldraste. Functionally incapacitated by Stillness though officially still head of house.
characters.physical_details: Level 300. Physically preserved but mentally deteriorating — the Stillness. Empty eyes, slowing speech, thinning personality. Sits with perfect posture and no presence. The body is maintained; the person is leaving.

characters.availability: Referenced from Ch 1 (unnamed) through Ch 16 (named). Physically present only in Ch 19.

key_relationships.vel_ashwick: lover — separated by the threshold for eight years

characters.occupation_status: Former deep-route explorer alongside Vel. Now a member and leader of the sub-Ledger community.
characters.physical_details: Not physically described until Ch 19. She crossed the threshold eight years before the story starts. In the sub-Ledger, she is older, changed — eight years of building a new life without abilities.

state_transitions.change: Reunited with Vel in the sub-Ledger

environmental_constants: The Ledger's background hum: all characters above zero perceive a subtle background sensation — awareness of their remaining levels, like a number always at the edge of consciousness. This sensation disappears in the sub-Ledger, and the absence is physical — described by arrivals as the loudest quiet they've ever heard.
The Vault's silence: the Hoarded capital is abnormally quiet. No raised voices, no sudden movements, no sounds of exertion. Architecture amplifies stillness.

The Spent Towns' working noise: the sound of abilities being used for labor — the hum of Fertility Charms, the crack of Shaping abilities, the warm pulse of healing. A constant low soundtrack of productive spending.

The Margins' exhaustion: depleted soil that does not respond to Fertility Charms anymore, crumbling structures showing the seams of cheap Shaping work, the visual and sensory landscape of overuse and abandonment. Also: the bureaucratic residue of Hoarded exploitation — posted notices, labor classifications, debt documents in procedural language.
The Scour's exposure: constant wind, layered rock visible in the erosion basin, no vegetation, intense sun. The terrain feels geologically naked — the earth's bones showing through.

The sub-Ledger's silence: the absence of the Ledger's background hum is the defining sensory experience. No number at the edge of consciousness. No potential energy stored in the body. The silence is physical — like a phantom limb in reverse, reaching for the Ledger and finding nothing.
Seasonal progression: late spring warmth in early chapters becoming full summer heat by the Margins and the Scour. The journey moves south into warmer, drier, more exposed territory.

income_sources.amount: Irregular, small payments in marks or barter
income_sources.reliability: Unreliable — depends on finding settlements that need help and can pay
Reliable when actively trading; suspended during the journey with Vel and Kem
Finite — once spent, no replacement. Used for Dren's payment and supplies.
income_sources.source: Kem's portable valuables from the Vault

distances: The Vault to the Spent Towns: approximately 8-10 days of travel on foot
The Spent Towns to Thessa's settlement: approximately 5-6 days south
Thessa's settlement to the Margins border: approximately 4-5 days
The Margins border to the Scour: approximately 8-10 days through depleted territory
Total journey (Vault to Scour): approximately 5-6 weeks

key_locations.details: Hoarded capital. Grand, immaculate, silent. Architecture designed to minimize the need for action — everything is maintained by institutional structures rather than individual ability use. The Conservatory is where weekly Rites of inaction are performed. Walled, gated, heavily administered. Contains a sealed wing housing the Red Reserve — a different quality of silence behind its doors.
key_locations.name: The Vault

key_locations.details: A loose network of self-governing settlements in the middle latitudes where people average level 150-200. Functional communities built through moderate ability use — Fertility Charms for farming, Shaping abilities for construction, healing for medicine. Trade routes connect them. They consider themselves practical; the Hoarded consider them lower class.
key_locations.name: The Spent Towns

key_locations.details: A specific Spent Town where Thessa has built her life. South of the main Spent Towns cluster, closer to the Margins border. Has Hoarded economic ties — representatives visit, including at least one affected by early-stage Stillness.
key_locations.name: Thessa's Settlement

key_locations.details: Depleted territory in the south where generations of people have been ground down by Hoarded exploitation. Average levels 15-30. Depleted farmland, crumbling infrastructure, poverty. The landscape shows overuse — soil exhausted by Fertility Charms, structures built fast and cheap. People are gaunt, careful, deeply suspicious. The bureaucratic residue of Hoarded exploitation is visible: labor classifications, debt documents, posted notices in procedural language that classified humans as Renewable Expenditure Units.
key_locations.name: The Margins

key_locations.details: A vast erosion basin at the southern extreme of the Margins. Wind-scoured, geologically exposed, layered rock visible. The terrain drops into a deep basin accessible by a single navigable descent (where the Red Reserve positions) or through collapsed mining tunnels. This is where the threshold to the sub-Ledger opens. The Red Reserve has maintained a permanent classified garrison here for years. The threshold has been deliberately weakened over three years of dampening.
key_locations.name: The Scour

key_locations.details: A world beneath the threshold, accessible only at zero. A civilization built over centuries by people who have been spent to nothing — involuntary zeros who have created agriculture, medicine, governance, and community entirely without abilities. Medicine uses obsidian-edge tools and herbalism through apprenticeship chains. Governance runs through a council of equals with tensions between voluntary and involuntary zeros. Architecture is hand-quarried stone showing tool marks — every surface shows labor. Not a paradise; a hard-built, politically complex human world. Its only protection has been secrecy.
key_locations.name: The Sub-Ledger

geography.primary_setting: A world governed by the Ledger system, stretching from the Vault (Hoarded capital in the north) through the Spent Towns and Margins to the Scour (threshold location in the far south).

power_structure.mechanism: Land ownership, trade route control, governance structures, debt instruments, and social hierarchy maintained over centuries of accumulated institutional power rather than direct ability use. Additionally: active suppression of the threshold at the Scour through the Red Reserve's permanent garrison and deliberate threshold dampening.
power_structure.over: The entire Ledger world through institutional and economic control, including suppression of the sub-Ledger's existence

power_structure.mechanism: Hereditary institutional authority; families maintain power through political alliances, land holdings, and the social prestige of complete preservation
De facto head of house due to Lord Aldraste's Stillness; manages the family's reputation and Council standing
power_structure.over: House Aldraste's political and economic affairs

power_structure.mechanism: Debt structures, indentured labor programs requiring ability use, trade route monopolies, and economic dependency. The Spent Towns are self-governing but economically subordinate. The Margins are effectively abandoned but were exploited for generations.
power_structure.over: Spent Towns and the Margins through economic control

power_structure.mechanism: Self-governance through community consensus; limited autonomy under Hoarded economic dominance

Council of equals with representation from both voluntary and involuntary zeros; consensus-based governance without ability-based coercion; internal tensions between the two populations over vision and priorities
power_structure.over: The sub-Ledger community — governance, resource allocation, infrastructure decisions

seasonal_track.season: Late spring — warming temperatures, long days, the landscape greening
Early summer — full heat, dry conditions in the Margins
Mid-summer — peak heat, the Scour is baked and exposed

timeline.story_start: Late spring, in a settlement south of the Vault. Vel has just completed a healing job.

world_rules: Everyone is born at level 300 with a full Ledger — all abilities unlocked at maximum potential.

Every ability use permanently and irreversibly reduces the user's level. The cost varies by ability tier: minor abilities (light, basic strength boost) cost 1-3 levels; moderate abilities (healing, combat skills, utility charms) cost 3-15 levels; major abilities (powerful combat, large-scale construction, complex healing) cost 15-40 levels; extraordinary abilities (massive defensive bursts, wide-area effects) cost 40-80 levels.
No mechanism exists to restore spent levels. Once a level is gone, it is permanent.
Basic physical functions — walking, eating, breathing, talking, sleeping, manual labor — do not cost levels. The Ledger governs enhanced abilities, not baseline human function.
Individuals can see their own Ledger internally — their current level and available abilities. Detecting another person's level requires a Perception or Detection ability, which costs levels to use.
The average person dies of natural causes at approximately level 280, having spent about 20 levels across a normal lifespan on practical tasks. Natural lifespans are similar to ours (70-90 years).

The Hoarded aristocracy preserves power through total inaction — never spending a single level. Their social and economic power comes from institutional control (land, trade routes, governance), not from ability use.

Long-term level-300 preservation correlates with a degenerative condition called Stillness — gradual mental deterioration characterized by empty eyes, slowing speech, and personality thinning. The Hoarded officially celebrate Stillness as the highest form of discipline. Unofficially, it is the system's dark secret. Stillness is portable: defectors who leave the Vault after prolonged preservation carry the damage with them.
Level zero is a threshold. When a person reaches zero in certain geographic locations (notably the Scour), they cross into the sub-Ledger — a separate world where the Ledger does not exist.

The threshold is more stable when multiple spenders approach zero simultaneously in proximity. A sustained cascade of spending — especially a high-to-zero descent — amplifies the threshold effect. One person can cross alone (unstable, unpredictable). Two co-spenders create a reliable crossing at an intact threshold. A degraded threshold may require three or more contributors.

The threshold can degrade over time through natural erosion of the cascade site and through deliberate dampening. The Hoarded have been dampening the Scour threshold for approximately three years, reducing its energy capacity. Vel's calculations, based on Maren's crossing eight years ago at an intact threshold, are outdated by the time of the story.

The Hoarded inner leadership has known about the threshold and the sub-Ledger for decades. The preservation philosophy is partly maintained to prevent people from reaching zero and discovering what lies beyond. The Red Reserve maintains a permanent classified garrison at the Scour to prevent crossings. This suppression is the Hoarded system's deepest secret — more closely guarded than the Red Reserve's existence or Stillness.

The Red Reserve is the Hoarded's secret unit of trained spenders — agents permitted to use abilities for security purposes. Their existence is concealed because it contradicts the Hoarded philosophy of total inaction. Reserve agents are at approximately levels 250-270, trained in combat. The Scour garrison commander has been stationed for five years and has witnessed three crossings.

The sub-Ledger has no Ledger. No abilities, no levels, no stats. People there live through baseline human capacity only. It is a real, physical place with its own civilization — centuries old, built by involuntary zeros with a later influx of voluntary zeros. Internal governance involves tensions between these two populations over vision and authority.
